changes.summary: The League's division standings finally look the part. Each division table is now topped by a branded blue banner: the division's own compass badge on the left (Northwest, Southwest, Central, East each get their own mark), the division name in big condensed caps, and the defending champion's logo riding shotgun on a white medallion right beside it. The old plain-text "Division Champions: …" line is gone — the hardware speaks for itself now.

The AFL team The Show has fresh artwork — a new graffiti-style avatar now shows up wherever the team appears across the site (standings, rosters, and the GroupMe chat), replacing the old placeholder mark.

The League's homepage hero and What's New page no longer pick up AFL-only announcements — an untagged AFL feature (the franchise trophy room) had been headlining The League's homepage. League tagging is now mandatory and fails closed, so cross-league mix-ups can't happen again.
The 'Roger and Schefter Suit Up for AFL' announcement is visible again on the AFL What's New page — a mistyped league tag had been silently hiding it from every site since late June.
